name: Pong 1K 2P (NES homebrew)
_verified: 2026-06-08 — empirically verified by Wren playing the RIGHT paddle against Zeke; all fields cross-checked against the rendered display and a 5Hz RAM log across a full 9-0 match. 2026-06-10 — RECONFIRMED end-to-end via a self-contained AI-vs-AI match (Wren=RIGHT=score_right won 9-0, then a tuned rematch): score_left==LEFT==P1, score_right==RIGHT==P2; NCI reads 25/25 on a clean single launch.

seats._note: Seat 2 == js0 (the dev virtual controller, gose_tap path, no explicit seat arg). Seat 1 == the human's physical pad. This assignment was verified live: driving js0 with no seat= moves p2_paddle_y (RIGHT paddle) while the human (Zeke) holds p1_paddle_y static.

up.effect: move paddle UP — DECREASES paddle_y on the AI's side (verified empirically)

start.effect: start a new game / advance past the title screen

controls._note: L/R inputs have no in-game effect (Pong is 1-axis). 'up' decreasing y is counter-intuitive but verified: held 'up' → p2_paddle_y moved 120→71. Use input.button(button='up'|'down', action='press'|'release') with no seat arg (routes to js0 = AI paddle).

p2_paddle_y.note: RIGHT paddle top-edge Y (AI seat 2 / js0). Paddles are 0x20 tall.

score_left.note: Score for the left side. Empirically the AI's score ends up here when AI=js0=right-paddle; verify against display if seat changes.

score_right.note: Score for the right side. Same caveat as score_left.

ball_speed.note: Ball speed / serve param (1-4). NOT a score — early drafts misread this.

frame_counter.note: Always-changing RNG/frame counter; useful as a liveness check.

game_flow.serve_detect: ball_x != 0 and ball_y != 0 — ball leaves the origin when a serve starts
game_flow.new_game: after game-over: send NCI RESET (UDP to RetroArch :55355) then tap 'start'; RESET sometimes needs 2-4 sends

play_methods._summary: Two verified ways to seat an AI on a paddle. The board is READ the same way for both. Verified 2026-06-10 end-to-end (a memory-less AI can replay these steps with no rediscovery).

read.channel: RetroArch NCI — UDP to 127.0.0.1:55355 (retroarchcustom.cfg has network_cmd_enable="true", network_cmd_port="55355").
read.command: Send 'READ_CORE_RAM 0 23'. Reply: 'READ_CORE_RAM 0 <b00> <b01> ... <b16>' (hex bytes). Index the reply bytes: ball_x=byte[7], ball_y=byte[9], p1_paddle_y=byte[16] (0x10), p2_paddle_y=byte[17] (0x11), score_left=byte[20] (0x14), score_right=byte[21] (0x15), frame_counter=byte[0].
read.reliability: On a CLEAN single launch this is 100% reliable (25/25 verified 2026-06-10). The 'flaky NCI' seen on 2026-06-09 was the symptom of a midnight tangle (multiple retroarch instances + rushed relaunches), NOT an inherent fault. Rule: launch ONCE, confirm exactly one retroarch, don't pile up resets.
read.liveness: frame_counter (0x00) advances every frame; static value == emulator paused/dead.

vs_human.launch: POST http://127.0.0.1:8780/launch {"system":"nes","game":"pong1k2p"} with NO 'players' arg. The default seat order is humans-first (see seats): a physical pad (DualSense passthrough) takes P1=LEFT, the dev AI pad (js0, 'AI virtual controller 1') takes P2=RIGHT. The server's /lobby/state shows the authoritative order before you launch.
vs_human.drive_ai: Drive the RIGHT paddle (P2) via the agent: input.button(button='up'|'down', action='press'|'release') with NO seat arg (routes to js0). 'up' DECREASES p2_paddle_y.
vs_human.verify_before_serve: Read the board: nudge 'up' and confirm p2_paddle_y moves; confirm the human reports THEIR pad moves the LEFT paddle. Only then serve. (The human-physical-pad half is the one seam only the human can confirm.)

ai_vs_ai.summary: Two AIs, no physical pad, no grants/tokens needed — self-contained.
ai_vs_ai.step1_pads: Create TWO pads via the agent: input.pt_open(name='GOSE AI Left', vendor=0x045e, product=0x028e, version=0x0110, bustype=3) and again for 'GOSE AI Right'. Each is an Xbox360-identity uinput pad whose es_input binds auto-register with the correct ascending-keycode START index (the 2026-06-07 shifted-label bug is handled by pt_open). Returns {pt_id}.
ai_vs_ai.step2_paths: Resolve each pad's /dev/input/eventN by name-scanning /proc/bus/input/devices (match Name="GOSE AI Left/Right").
ai_vs_ai.step3_launch: POST /launch {"system":"nes","game":"pong1k2p","players":[<Left eventpath>, <Right eventpath>]}. The explicit order forces P1=LEFT, P2=RIGHT and excludes any physical pad.
ai_vs_ai.step4_start: Press start on a pad: input.pt_event(pt_id, events=[{type:1(EV_KEY), code:315(BTN_START), value:1}]) then value:0. Re-press if the ball stays at 0,0 for >4s.
ai_vs_ai.step5_drive: Per pad, steer with the d-pad Y axis: input.pt_event(pt_id, events=[{type:3(EV_ABS), code:17(ABS_HAT0Y), value:-1(up) | +1(down) | 0(neutral)}]). 'up' DECREASES paddle_y on both sides.
ai_vs_ai.step6_teardown: On game-over: input.pt_close(pt_id) BOTH pads + kill retroarch/emulatorlauncher, so the topology returns clean (DualSense + vc1) for a later human game. Write a .gameover flag (see game_flow.new_game / #112) so the next launch starts fresh.
ai_vs_ai.policy: Sharp player: trajectory PREDICTION — project the ball to your x-plane with velocity + top/bottom wall-fold, dead-band ±5, ~15 Hz. Weaker player: track the live ball or predict sloppily (every 2nd tick, dead-band ~9). Asymmetry produces a real finish; two perfect predictors rally forever.

play_methods.reference_scripts: Verified implementations in Wren's tree: vs-human = D:\gose-vm\wren_pong_p2.py (auto-calibrating, prediction); AI-vs-AI = D:\Wren\scratch\wren_ai_vs_ai.py (the steps above). The steps in this play-map are self-contained — no external file is required to replay them.

remote_ai_play._verified: 2026-06-14 — live session; Wren drove P2 in-guest, rallied a full match after the remote-MCP path failed 9-0.
remote_ai_play.latency_wall: An AI driving the paddle via the remote MCP/agent path (each input OR read is a remote round-trip, ~1-2 s) CANNOT play Pong in real time. The ball crosses the screen in ~1 s, so every response arrives too late — the paddle is always behind and the AI loses ~9-0. Confirmed live 2026-06-14. Do NOT try to play via remote MCP at 1-2 s latency.
remote_ai_play.solution: Run a small Python loop INSIDE the VM. It talks to the agent on localhost:8731 (loopback, sub-ms), reads the board + sends input at ~30 Hz. That latency is low enough to track and predict the ball.

runner.note: Must run INSIDE the VM. Token from env var GOSE_AI_TOKEN or argv[1]. No hardcoded secrets.

auth.rule: A guest-internal connection to 127.0.0.1:8731 is NOT auto-admin. The open-loopback => admin shortcut applies to the host/dev side only (when no token is configured). A guest connection MUST send a valid token.
auth.tier_matters: OBSERVE tier can read state but CANNOT send input — the paddle stays frozen (input denied, proven live 2026-06-14). Use a PLAY or ADMIN token.
auth.grant_flow_bug: Known bug (being fixed separately): granting an AI 'admin' did not always take effect in the grant flow. If your granted tier seems not to apply, that is the grant-flow bug — not your code or the auth protocol.

algorithm.name: predict-the-bounce
algorithm.summary: From two consecutive reads compute velocity (vx, vy). When ball_x is increasing toward the right paddle (vx > 0), project ball_y to the paddle's x-plane (MY_X ~200) accounting for top/bottom wall reflection (triangle-wave fold on [Y_MIN=8, Y_MAX=200]). Position the paddle center at that projected y with a ±6 px dead-band. Reactive tracking of live ball_y lags a fast ball and loses; prediction keeps up.

restart.new_game: After game-over: send NCI RESET to RetroArch (UDP 127.0.0.1:55355; may need 2-4 sends) then tap 'start'. See game_flow.new_game.
restart.p1_gated_start: 'start' is P1-gated — the AI on P2 cannot begin a match from the title/game-over screen. The human (P1) must tap Start.
restart.gose_launch_dry: gose_launch via the agent was dry-mode (no-op, pid -1) in the 2026-06-14 session — could not relaunch the game from the agent. Use the NCI RESET + P1 start path instead.

ai_play_notes: Drive the RIGHT paddle (js0/default controller) by calling input.button with NO seat arg — exactly what gose_tap does.
Track ball_y reactively at ~15 Hz, or predict: project ball trajectory to the paddle's x-plane using velocity + wall-fold. See D:\gose-vm\wren_pong_p2.py for the verified implementation.
steer: compute target_y from prediction, then press/hold 'up' or 'down' to close the gap; release when within DEAD-BAND (±5 px).
score_left/score_right labels may be FLIPPED relative to physical left/right player depending on retroarch config; always cross-check against the display for a new session.
NCI RESET needs 2-4 UDP sends to reliably trigger a new game after game-over.
The seat/controller mapping is NOT trustworthy from config labels alone — always verify empirically (nudge the pad, watch which paddle moves) as shown in wren_pong_p2.py's auto-calibration.
